SEARCH

All Classes and Interfaces

Class
Description
AffineTraversal<S,A>
An optic that focuses on at most one element.
Applicative<W>
A typeclass for functors that support functional application.
Array<T>
 
Array.NonEmpty<T>
 
Bifunctor<W>
A typeclass for type constructors with two type arguments that support mapping over both.
CancellationToken
A token that can be used to request cancellation of an asynchronous operation.
Codec
Foundation for functional binary and text codecs.
Collection<T>
The root interface for all purely functional polymorphic collections.
Collection.µ
 
Const<A,B>
The constant functor, which holds a value of type A and ignores its second type argument B.
Const.µ
 
Decoder<A>
A functional decoder that reads a value from a DataInputStream.
Deque<T>
A purely functional Double-Ended Queue (Deque).
Deque.BankersDeque<T>
 
Either<A,B>
A data structure representing a value of one of two possible types.
Either.CheckedSupplier<T>
 
Either.Left<A,B>
 
Either.Right<A,B>
 
Encoder<A>
A functional encoder that writes a value to a DataOutputStream.
Endo<A>
Represent a function from a type to itself.
Eq<T>
A typeclass for types that support equality.
F3<A,B,C,R>
 
F4<A,B,C,D,R>
 
Fold<S,A>
A read-only optic that focuses on zero to many elements.
Functor<W>
A typeclass for types that support a mapping operation.
FunctorLaws
Utility to verify Functor laws.
Gen<A>
A generator for random data of type A.
Gen.µ
 
Generator
Utility for generating potentially infinite sequences of values.
Getter<S,A>
A read-only optic that focuses on exactly one element.
Graph<V>
Functional Graph implementation using adjacency maps.
Graph.AdjacencyGraph<V>
 
Hashable<T>
A typeclass for values that can be converted to a canonical, stable hash string.
HashMap<K,V>
A persistent Hash Array Mapped Trie (HAMT) implementation.
HashMap.CollisionNode
 
HashMap.Entry<K,V>
 
HashMap.HAMT<K,V>
 
HashMap.IndexedNode
 
HashMap.LeafNode
 
HashMap.Node
 
Higher<W,A>
A witness interface for Higher-Kinded Types in Java.
Identity<A>
The identity functor, which simply wraps a value.
Identity.µ
 
IO<A>
Represents a synchronous side-effecting computation that is deferred.
IO.µ
 
Iso<S,A>
An optic that represents a lossless, two-way transformation between types S and A.
JsonCodec<A>
A functional codec for JSON serialization and deserialization.
JsonParser
A purely functional JSON parser implementation.
JsonValue
A purely functional JSON AST with built-in optics.
JsonValue.JsonArray
 
JsonValue.JsonBoolean
 
JsonValue.JsonNull
 
JsonValue.JsonNumber
 
JsonValue.JsonObject
 
JsonValue.JsonString
 
Lazy<T>
A purely functional container for a deferred, memoized computation.
LazyList<T>
A lazy, potentially infinite persistent list.
LazyList.Empty<T>
 
LazyList.NonEmpty<T>
 
Lens<S,A>
An optic that focuses on a single mandatory field within a structure.
List<T>
A purely functional Linked List (Snoc-list style).
List.EmptyList<T>
 
List.LinkedList<T>
 
Map<K,V>
A purely functional Map interface.
Map.BinaryTreeMap<K,V>
 
Map.Entry<K,V>
 
Maybe<T>
A data structure representing an optional value.
Maybe.Nothing<T>
 
Maybe.Some<T>
 
Monad<W>
A typeclass for applicative functors that support sequential composition.
MonadLaws
Utility to verify Monad laws.
Monoid<T>
An algebraic structure consisting of a set together with a single binary operation and an identity element.
MonoidLaws
Utility to verify Monoid laws.
NonEmptyList<A>
A list that is guaranteed to have at least one element.
NonEmptyList.µ
 
Optics
Entry points for functional optics (Lens, Prism, Traversal).
Ord<T>
A typeclass for types that support ordering.
ParseError
Represents a failure in the parsing process.
Parser<A>
Foundation for purely functional parser combinators.
Pipe<F,I,O>
A stream transducer that transforms a stream of inputs to a stream of outputs.
PriorityQueue<T>
A purely functional Priority Queue implementation using a Skew Leftist Heap.
PriorityQueue.LeftistHeap<T>
 
Prism<S,A>
An optic that focuses on a case or a potentially missing field.
Profunctor<W>
A typeclass for type constructors with two type arguments that are contravariant in the first and covariant in the second.
Property<A>
A property that can be verified against generated data.
Queue<T>
A purely functional Queue (FIFO).
Queue.BankersQueue<T>
 
Reader<R,A>
Represents a computation that reads from an environment of type R.
Reader.µ
 
ReaderTaskEither<R,E,A>
The ultimate effect for modern applications.
ReaderTaskEither.µ
 
RecordComponentFunction<S,A>
A specialized Function that is Serializable, allowing us to extract metadata about the method reference at runtime.
RecordOptics
Automated Optics generation for Java Records using Method References.
Ref<A>
A functional reference cell that holds an immutable value and provides thread-safe, non-blocking updates using atomic Compare-And-Swap (CAS) operations.
RoseTree<T>
A purely functional Rose Tree (multi-way tree).
RoseTree.RoseTreeImpl<T>
 
SealedOptics
Automated Prism generation for Java 17+ Sealed Interfaces.
Semigroup<T>
An algebraic structure consisting of a set together with a single associative binary operation.
Set<T>
A purely functional Set implementation using a self-balancing AVL Tree.
Set.AVLTree<T>
 
Set.Empty<T>
 
Set.NonEmpty<T>
 
Shrink<A>
A typeclass for types that can be shrunk to "simpler" values.
Sink<F,I>
A stream consumer that performs an effect for each element in the stream.
Stack<T>
A purely functional Stack (LIFO).
Stack.StackImpl<T>
 
State<S,A>
Represents a computation that reads and writes to a state of type S.
State
Immutable state tracking the parsing position, including line and column.
State.µ
 
Stream<F,A>
A lazy, resource-safe data stream evaluated within an effect context F.
Task<A>
A purely functional abstraction for an asynchronous computation.
Task.µ
 
TaskEither<E,A>
Represents an asynchronous computation that can fail with an error of type E or succeed with a value of type A.
TaskEither.µ
 
These<A,B>
Represents values that can be either A, B, or Both A and B.
These.µ
 
These.Both<A,B>
 
These.That<A,B>
 
These.This<A,B>
 
Traversable<W>
A typeclass for functors that can be traversed from left to right, performing an action on each element.
Traversal<S,A>
An optic that focuses on 0 to N elements within a collection.
Tuple<A,B>
A purely functional Tuple (Pair) of two values.
Tuple.TupleImpl<A,B>
 
TypeReference<T>
A Super Type Token used to capture generic type information at runtime.
Validation<E,T>
A data type that represents either a success (Valid) or an accumulation of errors (Invalid).
Validation.Invalid<E,T>
 
Validation.Valid<E,T>
 
Vector<T>
Persistent Vector implementation using a Bitmapped Vector Trie.
Vector.VectorImpl<T>
 
Writer<W,A>
Represents a computation that produces a value of type A and a log of type W.
Writer.µ
